articles.title: Reopening Cannot Be Quashed Using New Law Retrospectively – Matter Remanded to CIT(A)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment notice issued within four years cannot be quashed using a new retrospective law. The matter was remanded to the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) for correct application of tax provisions. The decision underscores the importance of adhering to applicable laws in tax reassessments.
articles.raw_summary: The reassessment notice issued within four years was wrongly quashed by applying amended law. The Tribunal restored the matter, emphasizing correct application of applicable provisions. articles.title: GST ITC Denial on GSTR-2A Mismatch: Telangana HC Allows Rectification Route
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ruled that taxpayers denied input tax credit (ITC) due to mismatches with GSTR-2A can seek rectification by submitting supporting documents to the proper officer. The decision addresses disputes over ITC claims where invoices were missing or unmatched in the GST system. This impacts businesses facing ITC denials and clarifies the process for rectifying such discrepancies. The ruling provides a legal route for taxpayers to resolve compliance issues with tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The court addressed denial of input tax credit due to mismatch with GSTR-2A and missing invoices. It allowed the taxpayer to seek rectification with supporting documents before the proper officer. articles.title: Telangana HC Rejects Vires Challenge to Section 16(2)(c); Permits Delayed GST Appeal
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court rejected a constitutional challenge to Section 16(2)(c) of the GST Act, which pertains to input tax credit conditions. The court permitted delayed GST appeals to be filed with condonation of delay, subject to conditions. This ruling affects taxpayers seeking to challenge GST-related decisions beyond standard timelines. The decision clarifies legal recourse for delayed GST appeals under Indian tax law.
articles.raw_summary: The court refused to entertain a constitutional challenge raised to bypass limitation. It allowed filing of appeal with delay condonation subject to conditions. The post Telangana HC Rejects Vires Challenge to Section 16(2)(c); Permits Delayed GST Appeal appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Excess ITC Case: Telangana HC Declines Vires Challenge to Section 16(2)(c)
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court declined to examine the constitutional validity of Section 16(2)(c) of the tax law due to the absence of a clear factual basis. The court held that disputed facts should be addressed through statutory appellate remedies rather than a direct challenge to the provision's validity. The case pertains to excess Input Tax Credit (ITC) claims under India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework.
articles.raw_summary: The court declined to examine the constitutional validity of Section 16(2)(c) in absence of clear factual basis. articles.title: Demonetised Cash from Members Not Unexplained: ITAT Upholds Deletion under Section 68
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) upheld the deletion of unexplained cash deposits under Section 68, ruling that demonetized cash from members of an entity cannot be treated as unexplained if the source is properly explained. The tribunal condoned an 820-day delay due to a bona fide jurisdictional mistake. This decision impacts tax assessment procedures and clarifies the treatment of member contributions in income tax cases.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT condoned an 820-day delay due to a bona fide jurisdictional mistake. It held that SBN deposits from members cannot be treated as unexplained when the source is properly explained. articles.title: Credit Card Payments Explained – Addition u/s 69C Deleted in Absence of Unexplained Source
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled in favor of an assessee after reviewing affidavits, bank statements, and financial records of contributors. The tribunal deleted a tax addition under Section 69C of the Income Tax Act, stating that the source of funds was satisfactorily explained. This decision impacts tax assessment practices and clarifies the burden of proof in unexplained credit cases. The ruling was reported by TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal noted that the assessee provided affidavits, bank statements, and financials of contributors. The addition was deleted as the source stood satisfactorily explained. articles.title: ITC Not Blocked on Leasehold Transfers Without Construction: HC Quashes GST Notice
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court ruled that Input Tax Credit (ITC) on Goods and Services Tax (GST) Development Charges (GIDC) is allowable even when no construction is involved in leasehold transfers. The court also quashed a GST notice against ITC, stating that Section 74 proceedings (which penalize tax evasion) cannot proceed without evidence of fraud or suppression. The ruling impacts businesses and tax authorities interpreting GST rules on leasehold transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court ruled that ITC on GIDC charges is allowable where no construction is involved. It also held that Section 74 proceedings fail without fraud or suppression. The post ITC Not Blocked on Leasehold Transfers Without Construction: HC Quashes GST Notice appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 68 Addition for Share Premium Addition Deleted due to Limited Scrutiny Breach
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the Assessing Officer (AO) exceeded their authority by invoking Section 68 of the Income Tax Act without prior approval during a limited scrutiny assessment. As a result, the tribunal deleted the addition of share premium and quashed the assessment as legally unsustainable. The decision highlights the importance of adhering to procedural limits in tax scrutiny.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that the AO exceeded the scope of limited scrutiny by invoking Section 68 without prior approval. The assessment was quashed as legally unsustainable, and the addition was deleted. The post Section 68 Addition for Share Premium Addition Deleted due to Limited Scrutiny Breach appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Reassessment Quashed – Invalid Sanction by PCIT Instead of PCCIT Beyond 3 Years
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed a reassessment order because it was approved by the wrong authority (PCIT instead of PCCIT) beyond the three-year limit. The ruling highlights that higher-level approval is mandatory for such reassessments, and the incorrect sanction led to the entire process being invalidated. This decision impacts tax administration and compliance for future cases involving reassessment beyond three years.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT held that reassessment beyond three years requires approval from the higher authority, not PCIT. Since approval was wrongly obtained, the entire reassessment was quashed. The post Reassessment Quashed – Invalid Sanction by PCIT Instead of PCCIT Beyond 3 Years appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Gujarat HC Quashed GST Order Due to Denial of Section 75(4) Personal Hearing
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court set aside a GST order against a taxpayer because the tax authorities denied them a personal hearing under Section 75(4) of the GST law. The court ruled that ignoring the taxpayer’s additional reply and hearing request made the order unsustainable. The case has been sent back for reconsideration with proper procedural opportunities. The ruling underscores the importance of adhering to due process in tax adjudication.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling highlights that ignoring a taxpayer’s additional reply and request for hearing renders the order unsustainable. articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court stayed an Election Commission order directing officials to take preventive action against certain citizens labeled as 'troublemakers' during the Bengal polls. The court ruled that officials had erred by issuing a blanket direction that treated specific citizens as troublemakers without justification. This decision highlights concerns over arbitrary state action and its impact on citizens' rights during elections.
articles.raw_summary: Officials had ‘erred in issuing a blanket direction by treating certain citizens as troublemakers’, the court said.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that pre-purchase payments made towards a new property can be counted towards the total investment for claiming full capital gains exemption under Section 54. articles.title: SC seeks Centre’s response to petition challenging NIA Act
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the central government to respond to a public interest litigation that challenges the National Investigation Agency (NIA) Act. The petition argues that the Act is arbitrary, lacks legislative competence, and encroaches on state powers. The NIA Act empowers a central agency to investigate crimes, raising federalism concerns. The court's directive marks a key step in the legal review of the Act.
articles.raw_summary: The public interest litigation described the Act as arbitrary, lacking in legislative competence and an encroachment on state power.

articles.title: Search on Others Can’t Extend Reassessment Limit Without Assessee-Specific Seized Material
articles.summary: The court ruled that the Income Tax Department cannot extend the reassessment limit beyond six years without seizing assessee-specific material. The notice issued after six years from the relevant assessment year was deemed barred by limitation, and the reassessment proceedings were set aside. This decision limits the department's ability to reopen older tax cases unless specific seized evidence exists. The ruling has implications for tax administration and reassessment timelines in India.

articles.title: Section 263 Quashed in Search Case – PCIT Cannot Revise Without Touching U/s 153D Approval
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that the Principal Commissioner of Income Tax (PCIT) cannot exercise revisionary powers under Section 263 of the Income Tax Act without challenging the statutory approval granted under Section 153D. articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] cannot dismiss an appeal simply because the taxpayer did not prosecute it. articles.title: India assured DTAA tax benefits will continue after Supreme Court ruling, Mauritius Cabinet Note says
articles.summary: India has assured Mauritius that it will uphold benefits under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement. This comes after a Supreme Court ruling raised concerns. India has softened tax regulations regarding the General Anti-Avoidance Rule for older investments. This move aims to provide certainty to foreign investors and private equity funds on tax matters.

articles.title: Section 68 Addition Deleted in Search Case – No Incriminating Material Dumb Documents Rejected
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that statements used to add tax liability under Section 68 were later retracted and lacked corroboration, meaning they cannot solely justify additions. The ruling emphasizes the need for supporting evidence in tax proceedings. The case involved documents deemed 'dumb' (non-incriminating) being rejected. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle search cases and evidence in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal noted that statements relied upon were later retracted and lacked corroboration. It held that such statements cannot form sole basis of addition. The ruling emphasizes need for supporting evidence in tax proceedings. The post Section 68 Addition Deleted in Search Case – No Incriminating Material Dumb Documents Rejected appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Grama Nyaylaya Can't Exercise Jurisdiction Over Maintenance Proceedings In Areas Where Family Court Is Established: Kerala High Court
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court has held that Gram Nyayalayas established under the Gram Nyayalayas Act 2008, cannot exercise jurisdiction over maintenance proceedings under Chapter IX Cr.P.C (pertaining to orders for maintenance of wife, children and parents) in areas where a Family Court has been established. In doing so the court held that since Family Courts Act which is a special law, has not expressly altered by the Gram Nyayalayas Act, therefore provisions of the prior will prevail.� Justice K Babu

articles.title: ITAT Remands TDS Case as Assessee Not Given Opportunity to Submit Form 15G/15H Evidence
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) remanded a Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) case after finding that the assessee was not given an opportunity to submit Form 15G/15H evidence. The tribunal set aside prior orders and directed a fresh examination of compliance documents. This ruling highlights procedural fairness in tax adjudication and may impact future TDS compliance enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that failure to consider relevant documents denied proper adjudication. It set aside the orders and allowed fresh examination of compliance evidence. articles.title: Liquidated Damages vs. “Tolerating an Act” under GST – Reframing the Debate through Substance
articles.summary: The article explains the distinction between liquidated damages and compensation under India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime. It argues that liquidated damages are generally not subject to GST unless directly linked to a supply of goods or services. The discussion reframes the debate by emphasizing the need to differentiate between compensation for breach of contract and consideration for a supply, which has implications for tax compliance and policy interpretation.
articles.raw_summary: Explains why liquidated damages are generally not subject to GST unless linked to a supply. Highlights the importance of distinguishing compensation from consideration. The post Liquidated Damages vs. “Tolerating an Act” under GST   Reframing the Debate through Substance appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST Applies to Commission in Voucher Transactions, Not Entire Turnover: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that Goods and Services Tax (GST) applies only to commission income in voucher transactions, not the entire turnover. The court held that vouchers are not goods or services and thus cannot be taxed as supply. The matter has been sent back for fresh adjudication. This ruling limits GST liability to commission income where applicable.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling held that vouchers are not goods or services and cannot be taxed as supply. The matter was remanded for fresh adjudication, limiting GST liability to commission income where applicable. The post GST Applies to Commission in Voucher Transactions, Not Entire Turnover: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

This move aims to address potential risks from advanced AI systems and ensure preparedness in the financial sector.

articles.title: Repo rate hike not on the cards, for now, says Ram Singh, external member of MPC
articles.summary: Monetary Policy Committee member Ram Singh stated that a repo rate hike is not expected in the near term. He highlighted that inflation pressures may moderate if the West Asia conflict resolves and noted that India's foreign exchange reserves are adequate despite potential import cost increases. Singh also mentioned that open market operations will continue to manage system liquidity, while emphasizing India's strong domestic growth fundamentals despite possible supply shocks.
articles.raw_summary: Monetary Policy Committee member Ram Singh outlines the conditions for second-round inflation effects. He expects the West Asia conflict to resolve soon, moderating price rises. Forex reserves are considered adequate despite potential import cost increases. Open market operations will continue to ensure system liquidity. Domestic growth fundamentals remain robust despite potential supply shocks.

articles.title: Bogus Purchase Addition Restricted to 15% – Entire Disallowance Held Unsustainable
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that when sales are accepted as genuine, only 15% of the purchase value can be taxed as profit if purchases are deemed bogus under Section 69C. The tribunal held that an entire disallowance of purchases is unsustainable. This decision impacts tax assessments and compliance for businesses facing scrutiny over alleged bogus transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether entire purchases can be disallowed as bogus under Section 69C. The tribunal held that when sales are accepted, only the profit element (15%) can be taxed, not the full purchase value. The post Bogus Purchase Addition Restricted to 15% – Entire Disallowance Held Unsustainable appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: What Telangana’s survey shows about caste inequality
articles.summary: A survey in Telangana reveals that caste-based inequality is not just incremental but exponential, with Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) households remaining structurally disadvantaged despite economic growth. The findings underscore deep-rooted social disparities that persist in India. The data suggests systemic challenges that may require policy interventions to address caste-based inequities in access to resources and opportunities.

Iran's actions could disrupt global oil supplies, impacting India's energy security and economic stability.
articles.raw_summary: While the US is signaling a desire to negotiate, Iranians are playing for time — counting on the world's dependence on Persian Gulf energy exports and their strongest weapon, the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Lucknow ruled that additions made under Section 50C of the Income Tax Act are invalid if the Assessing Officer (AO) fails to follow the prescribed valuation procedure, specifically by not seeking a reference to the District Valuation Officer (DVO). The tribunal favored the taxpayer (assessee) due to the AO's non-compliance with the mandatory DVO reference process. articles.title: ITAT Pune Allowed Exemption Despite Delay in Form 10/10B Filing
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that delayed filing of Form 10/10B does not invalidate exemption claims under section 11(2) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal emphasized that procedural delays should not defeat substantive rights, allowing the exemption despite the delay. The decision impacts tax exemptions for trusts and non-profits in India.

articles.title: Striped hyena gets stronger global protection
articles.summary: The striped hyena has been granted stronger global protection under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals (CMS) treaty. This decision was made at the 15th Meeting of the Conference of the Parties (CMS CoP15). The species' conservation now requires greater cooperation among countries where it is found, including India. The treaty aims to protect migratory species threatened by habitat loss and human-wildlife conflict.
articles.raw_summary: The striped hyena has been given stronger international protection under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals treaty (CMS), with its conservation requiring greater cooperation among range states. This decision was made at the 15th Meeting of the Conference of the Parties to the CMS Convention (CMS CoP15), held in Campo [ ]

articles.summary: A court ruled that an ex-parte GST order passed without giving the taxpayer an opportunity to reply to a show cause notice is invalid. The matter has been sent back for fresh consideration, ensuring the taxpayer can now respond. This decision reinforces procedural fairness in tax adjudication under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The court held that an adjudication order passed without considering the taxpayer’s reply is unsustainable. This ruling impacts taxpayers who may need to correct mistakes in their filings. The clarification was highlighted in a post by TaxGuru, emphasizing the legal boundaries for revised returns.
articles.raw_summary: A taxpayer could submit a revised return u/s 139(5) only when it discovered a bona fide omission or incorrect statement in the original return submitted u/s 139(1). The post Revised ITR u/s 139(5) was allowed only for errors in the original return appeared first on TaxGuru .

The discussion is framed in the context of geopolitical tensions and the growing threat to digital assets, which could have broader implications for national security and data

articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that transportation and insurance charges must be included in the assessable value for excise duty when goods are delivered at the buyer’s premises under door delivery terms. The decision clarifies that such charges form part of the transaction value. The ruling impacts how businesses calculate excise duty liabilities. The case was decided by a tribunal, not a court, but carries significant legal weight for tax compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether transportation and insurance charges should be excluded from assessable value. articles.title: More countries are turning to nuclear power 40 years after the Chernobyl disaster
articles.summary: Over 400 nuclear reactors are currently operational in 31 countries, with about 70 more under construction. Nuclear power generates around 10% of the world's electricity and accounts for a quarter of all low-carbon power sources. The article highlights a resurgence in nuclear energy adoption 40 years after the Chernobyl disaster. While not specific to India, the global shift toward nuclear power may influence India's energy policy and climate strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Over 400 nuclear reactors are operational in 31 countries, while about 70 more are under construction. Nuclear power accounts for producing about 10% of the world's electricity, equivalent to about a quarter of all sources of low-carbon power.

articles.title: Government to bring new seed, pesticide laws next Parliament session, says Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan
articles.summary: The Indian government, led by Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan, will introduce new laws for seeds and pesticides in the next Parliament session. These laws aim to penalize fake farm inputs and improve access to quality seeds and credit for farmers. The government is also prioritizing region-specific agricultural planning to address food security, higher farm incomes, better nutrition, and potential water shortages.
articles.raw_summary: New laws for seeds and pesticides are nearing completion. These laws aim to curb fake farm inputs with stricter penalties. The government is also focusing on region-specific agricultural planning. Priorities include food security, higher farm incomes, and better nutrition. Farmers will benefit from improved access to quality seeds and credit. Planning will also consider potential water shortages.

articles.title: Face-off between MNS and Shiv Sena leader Nirupam over mandatory Marathi for autorickshaw and taxi drivers

articles.title: Supreme Court Issues Notice On Christian Michel James' Plea Against Extradition Treaty Clause
articles.summary: The Supreme Court today issued notice on a plea filed by AgustaWestland VVIP chopper scam accused Christian Michel James against the Delhi High Court's dismissal of his petition challenging Article 17 of the 1999 India-UAE extradition treaty. A bench of CJI Surya Kant, Justice Joymalya Bagchi and Justice Vipul Pancholi passed the order after hearing Advocate Aljo K Joseph . The matter has been listed before a bench led by Justice Vikram Nath . To recap, Michel was extradited to India from Dubai

articles.summary: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has reminded trial courts that the propriety of judicial conduct and temper in addressing the Bar is a non-negotiable aspect of judicial grace, particularly when senior advocates with long-standing experience and due sense of responsibility are representing litigants. The findings suggest opportunities and challenges that could influence India's policy priorities in energy and technology sectors.

This ruling has implications for criminal law and the rights of family members in such offences.

articles.title: State capex hits 2.7% of GDP under SASCI scheme: Report

articles.raw_summary: The SASCI scheme has boosted state capital spending to 2.7% of GDP. However, utilization of central loans for capital expenditure is uneven across states. articles.title: Polycotton Recycling Faces a Scale Test That Chemistry Alone Cannot Solve
articles.summary: The article examines the challenges of recycling polycotton blended textiles, framing it as an industrial systems problem rather than just a scientific one. Peter Mangnus of Avantium argues that solving this requires aligning feedstock, offtake, finance, and policy support. The debate underscores the need for regulatory and infrastructure interventions to address textile waste at scale. The discussion is relevant to India's textile sector and waste management policies.
articles.raw_summary: Blended textile waste is no longer being discussed as a marginal recycling problem but as an industrial systems challenge shaped by regulation, cost and infrastructure. In that debate, Peter Mangnus , Business Director Dawn Technology at Avantium , argues that progress will depend less on scientific novelty alone than on aligning feedstock, offtake, finance and policy support.

articles.title: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
articles.summary: The US State Department has issued a global warning about alleged theft of AI intellectual property by Chinese firms, including DeepSeek, accusing them of extracting and replicating advanced US AI models. The concerns aim to counter low-cost replication of AI technology. China has denied these allegations. The issue highlights growing tensions over AI technology transfer and intellectual property rights in the global tech landscape.
articles.raw_summary: The US State Department is urging global attention to Chinese firms, including DeepSeek, accused of stealing AI intellectual property. A diplomatic cable details concerns over "adversaries' extraction and distillation of U.S. A.I. models," aiming to counter low-cost replication of advanced AI. China denies these allegations.

articles.title: 'Service Inam' Land Attached To Mosque Is Wakf Property, Can't Be Alienated : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that lands attached to mosques, known as 'service inam,' are part of Wakf property and cannot be sold or transferred. A bench led by Justice MM Sundresh and Justice Augustine George Masih stated that such lands, granted for religious or charitable purposes, are endowed property and subject to public or religious trust restrictions. The court emphasized that these lands are not alienable, reinforcing protections for Wakf properties under Indian law.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Friday (April 24) has observed that lands attached to Mosques known as 'service imams' form part of the Wakf Property, and therefore cannot be alienated. “It is undisputed and settled that lands granted as service inam for religious or charitable purposes partake the character of endowed property and are impressed with a public or religious trust, thereby restricting their alienability.” , observed a bench of Justice MM Sundresh and Justice Augustine George Masih. The Court

articles.title: Ancient glaciers reveal the roots of antimicrobial resistance
articles.summary: Researchers have found antimicrobial resistance (AMR) genes in microorganisms preserved in ancient glaciers and permafrost, suggesting resistance predates modern antibiotic use. This discovery highlights the natural origins of AMR and its long-term presence in ecosystems. The findings could inform India's strategies to combat AMR, a growing public health challenge in the country.

Microorganisms and free DNA, that have been preserved for thousands, even millions, of years, beneath the layers of ancient glacier ice, have been found to carry antimicrobial resistance genes. Officials noted this movement as part of ongoing wildlife migration between the two states. The incident underscores the need for cross-state coordination in wildlife conservation and habitat management.

articles.title: ‘Court not theatre of perception’—Justice Sharma’s order refusing Kejriwal’s recusal plea in excise case
articles.summary: Justice Sharma of the Delhi High Court refused Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse from the excise policy case, stating that unsubstantiated claims of bias cannot be allowed to undermine judicial independence. The judge emphasized that the judiciary cannot be put on trial based on perceptions alone, setting a precedent for institutional integrity. The order underscores the court's commitment to maintaining public trust in the justice system.
articles.raw_summary: The judge said that apprehension of bias, unsupported by tangible evidence, cannot be allowed to put judiciary itself on trial, or set a precedent that undermines institutional integrity.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley: Agentic AI shifts value from GPUs to CPUs and memory, creating up to $60bn incremental CPU TAM by 2030

This transition could create up to $60 billion in incremental CPU market opportunities by 2030. The report suggests these components will become new bottlenecks as AI moves beyond single-task generation to autonomous, multi-step operations.
articles.raw_summary: The next phase of artificial intelligence is no longer just about faster GPUs. As AI moves from single-task generation to autonomous, multi-step "agentic" systems, the economic value is migrating to the broader infrastructure stack, with CPUs and memory emerging as the new bottlenecks, Morgan Stanley said in its latest research report.

articles.title: Mundathikode fireworks accident: The spark that turned fatal
articles.summary: On April 21, an explosion at a fireworks unit in Mundathikode, Kerala, killed at least 12 people, injured over 24, and damaged 50 nearby houses. The unit, located in paddy fields, stored large quantities of explosive materials, raising concerns about safety violations. Investigations are underway into the cause of the blast. The tragedy underscores recurring risks in India's hazardous fireworks industry.
articles.raw_summary: On April 21, a deadly explosion at a fireworks unit at Mundathikode claimed at least 12 lives, injured more than 24, and damaged at least 50 houses nearby. The unit was set amid vast paddy fields and housed multiple sheds storing large quantities of explosive materials. Investigations are under way. Mini Muringatheri and Aathira Haridas report on the latest tragedy in a hazardous trade where violations are common

Companies like Xpeng are targeting over 50% of their revenue from outside China within a decade, including advanced technologies like flying cars and robotaxis. This push reflects China's ambition but also highlights harsh domestic economic conditions driving the overseas expansion.
articles.raw_summary: China's automotive industry is aggressively expanding overseas due to a saturated domestic market and a fierce price war. With a significant increase in car exports, companies like Xpeng are targeting global growth, aiming for over 50% of their revenue to come from outside China within a decade, including advanced technologies like flying cars and robotaxis.

articles.title: Amazon India plans to invest Rs 2,800 crore to strengthen operations; to focus on quick commerce
articles.summary: Amazon India announced an investment of Rs 2,800 crore to bolster its infrastructure and operations, aiming to enhance delivery speeds across the country. This investment will double the footprint of its quick commerce vertical, Amazon Now, and expand its pan-India ecommerce network, including warehouses and delivery stations.
articles.raw_summary: Amazon India announced an investment of Rs 2,800 crore to bolster its infrastructure and operations, aiming to enhance delivery speeds across the country. articles.title: Consumer watchdog group files suit alleging Meta profited on ads for scams
articles.summary: A U.S. consumer watchdog group, the Consumer Federation of America, has filed a lawsuit against Meta, alleging the company profited from scam advertisements on its platforms while misleading users about its safety efforts. Internal documents cited in the lawsuit suggest Meta earns billions from such ads despite claiming to combat fraud. Meta has denied the allegations and stated it will contest the lawsuit.
articles.raw_summary: Consumer Federation of America has sued Meta, accusing the social media giant of profiting from scam ads and misleading users about safety efforts. Internal documents suggest Meta earns billions from such ads, despite claims of combating fraud. Meta denies the allegations, stating they will contest the lawsuit.

articles.title: India's wheat output likely at 110-120 mn ton in 2025-26 amid weather damage, says Food Secy
articles.summary: India's wheat output for the 2025-26 crop year is expected to be between 110 and 120 million tonnes. Unseasonal rains and hailstorms have impacted crops in key growing states. The government has increased its wheat procurement target to 34.5 million tonnes. Exports are permitted but facing slow shipments. A new open market sale scheme policy is planned soon.

articles.title: India growth expected at 6.5% as country 'most directly impacted' by West Asia crisis: Gita Gopinath
articles.summary: India's economy is projected to grow at 6.5% this year, says Former IMF Deputy Managing Director Gita Gopinath to ET NOW. Global risks, particularly from West Asia, are impacting growth. Inflation is currently managed as companies absorb costs, but this may not last. Food prices could rise if disruptions continue. Central banks might delay interest rate cuts. Oil shocks will affect many economic sectors.

articles.title: Forecast for strong El Nino fans worries about global crops as Iran war bites
articles.summary: A strong El Nino is expected in the second half of 2026. This will bring hotter, drier weather to Asia. Crops and food supplies will be affected. Farmers face fertiliser shortages and high fuel costs. India anticipates below-average monsoon rains. Southeast Asia's palm oil and rice production may decline. China could see increased flooding risk.

articles.title: India’s private sector gains momentum in April on manufacturing strength
articles.summary: India's private sector activity gained pace in April. Manufacturing led the growth with increased output and new orders. Businesses expanded capacity and invested in technology. Companies built buffer stocks to manage supply chain uncertainties. Employment growth also picked up. Despite rising costs for fuel and raw materials, firms anticipate future output growth. Business confidence remained high.

articles.title: Iran war, oil shock fail to dent India’s private sector growth as April PMI rises
articles.summary: Indian businesses saw activity PMI grow in April. Manufacturing and services rebounded strongly. This happened even with rising costs from the Middle East conflict. Domestic demand remains robust. Companies are building stocks for future uncertainties. Employment is also increasing. This shows resilience despite global challenges.

articles.title: HSBC downgrades India to 'underweight' as oil clouds outlook
articles.summary: HSBC has downgraded Indian stocks to underweight. Rising energy prices from the Middle East war are a concern. This could impact India's earnings recovery. The market now looks less attractive compared to North East Asian peers. Foreign investors have been selling Indian stocks. Opportunities exist in select sectors like private banks, base metals, and healthcare.

articles.title: Smartphones emerge as India's export champions in FY26
articles.summary: India's electronics exports saw a significant 24% rise in the last fiscal year. Smartphones led this growth, with exports climbing nearly 22%. This surge highlights the success of the government's production linked incentive program. Key export destinations included the US, UAE, and China. This performance demonstrates India's growing role as a global manufacturing hub for electronics.

articles.title: India can grow above 7 pc even if crude oil costs USD 90-100/barrel: Assocham
articles.summary: India's economy shows resilience to high oil prices. Consumption drives growth, allowing over 7 percent annual expansion even with crude oil at USD 90-100 per barrel. Historical data supports this trend. Assocham predicts continued strong growth above 7 percent through 2026-27. This outlook contrasts with some international forecasts predicting moderation.

articles.title: Net sales growth of FDI companies moderated to 8.7% in FY25: RBI data
articles.summary: Net sales growth for select FDI companies in India moderated to 8.7% in 2024-25, down from 9.4% the previous year, according to RBI data. While services sector sales saw a slight increase, manufacturing sector growth decelerated. Operating profit growth also slowed to 10.7% due to rising expenses.

While services sector sales saw a slight increase, manufacturing sector growth decelerated. Operating profit growth also slowed to 10.7% due to rising expenses.

articles.title: Agriculture exports up 2.8 pc to USD 52.55 bn in 2025-26
articles.summary: India's agriculture exports are set to reach USD 52.55 billion in 2025-26, a 2.8% increase. This growth is fueled by traditional strengths and new high-value products. Marine and plantation exports are showing strong momentum. Pulses, fresh fruits, and vegetable oils are key contributors. Processed food segments are also expanding, highlighting value addition's role in boosting exports.

articles.title: Sumitomo Mitsui infuses Rs 1,075 cr into NBFC arm
articles.summary: Japanese conglomerate Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group has injected ₹1,075 crore into its Indian arm, SMFG India Credit. This follows a significant ₹3,000 crore infusion in December 2024. SMFG India Credit's assets under management reached ₹64,100 crore by December 2025. The group also increased its stake in Yes Bank to 24.2% in September 2025.

articles.title: Punjab activist climbs down after 18 months atop 400-ft BSNL tower as anti-sacrilege law notified
articles.summary: Activist Gurjeet Singh Khalsa ended a 560-day protest atop a 400-foot BSNL tower in Punjab's Samana after the state notified the Jaagat Jot Sri Guru Granth Sahib Satkar Amendment Act 2026, which strengthens penalties for sacrilege. The law's notification followed prolonged public outrage over alleged desecration incidents. Khalsa's protest, which began in 2024, was a demand for justice in sacrilege cases. The new legislation marks a significant policy response to address religious sensitivities

The analysis highlights ongoing supply bottlenecks as a key factor influencing global oil prices.

articles.title: Crude prices rise for fifth day with Brent crossing $106 as Iran talks stall amidst rhetoric
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have risen for five consecutive days, with Brent crossing $106 per barrel, amid stalled Iran nuclear talks and the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The US has asserted control over the region, stating it will destroy any ships suspected of planting mines. The situation involves Iran, the US, and regional stakeholders, with potential global energy supply disruptions. India, a major oil importer, may face economic and policy implications due to these developments.
articles.raw_summary: The Strait of Hormuz continues to remain closed with both sides claiming control of the narrow, crucial energy chokepoint. Trump continued to assert that US has complete control of the region and that he has instructed the US navy to blow up any ship that is seen planting mines in the region.

He expects prices to remain supported depending on supply disruptions, with investments likely to pick up across shale and offshore projects, alongside opportunities in oil companies and services.

articles.title: Oil prices extend gains as US-Iran peace negotiations stall

The surge follows stalled peace negotiations between the US and Iran, which could disrupt global oil supplies. This matters for India as it imports over 80% of its oil needs, potentially affecting fuel prices and inflation. The situation may prompt government responses like adjusting fuel subsidies or tapping strategic reserves.
